description: Kafka monitoring dashboard using the OpenTelemetry kafkametricsreceiver. Covers broker health, consumer group lag, topic configuration, and partition replication status.
title: Kafka Server Monitoring

widgets.description: Total number of brokers in the Kafka cluster. A drop in broker count indicates a broker has gone offline.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Broker Count

widgets.description: Log retention period configured per broker. Determines how long messages are kept before deletion.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Broker Log Retention Period

widgets.description: Total consumer lag aggregated per consumer group and topic. High lag indicates consumers are falling behind producers.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Consumer Group Lag by Topic

widgets.description: Number of active members in each consumer group. A sudden drop may indicate consumer crashes or rebalancing.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Consumer Group Members

widgets.description: Current committed offset per consumer group and topic. Tracks consumer progress through the topic.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Consumer Group Offset by Topic

widgets.description: Per-partition consumer lag for detailed troubleshooting. Identifies specific partitions causing consumer delays.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Consumer Lag by Partition

widgets.description: Number of partitions assigned to each topic. Partition count affects parallelism and throughput.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Partitions per Topic

widgets.description: Replication factor per topic. Values below the configured minimum indicate under-replicated topics.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Replication Factor

widgets.description: Minimum number of in-sync replicas required for each topic. Writes are rejected if ISR count falls below this value.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Min In-Sync Replicas

widgets.description: How long messages are retained per topic before being eligible for deletion.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Topic Log Retention Period

widgets.description: Maximum log size per topic before old segments are deleted. -1 means unlimited.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Topic Log Retention Size

widgets.description: Latest offset (head) of each partition, aggregated by topic. Shows overall write progress.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Partition Current Offset

widgets.description: Oldest available offset (tail) per partition, aggregated by topic. Gap between current and oldest indicates retained message volume.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Partition Oldest Offset

widgets.description: Total number of partition replicas per topic. Should match partitions × replication_factor.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Total Replicas per Topic

widgets.description: Number of in-sync replicas per topic. Should equal total replicas when cluster is healthy.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: In-Sync Replicas per Topic

widgets.description: Partitions where in-sync replicas < total replicas. Any value above zero indicates a replication problem that needs attention.

filter.expression: deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name
deployment.environment IN $deployment.environment AND kafka.cluster.alias IN $kafka.cluster.alias AND service.name = $service.name

widgets.title: Under-Replicated Partitions
